info.description:
Reconciliation engine for the Lerian Studio ecosystem.
Provides automated transaction matching between Midaz ledger and external systems.
info.title: Matcher Reconciliation API

tags.name: Configuration Contexts
tags.description: Reconciliation context management - create, update, delete contexts

tags.name: Configuration Sources
tags.description: Reconciliation source management - external data sources to match against

tags.name: Configuration Field Maps
tags.description: Field mapping configuration - define how source fields map to transactions

tags.name: Configuration Match Rules
tags.description: Match rule configuration - define matching algorithms and tolerances

tags.name: Ingestion
tags.description: File upload and import job management

tags.name: Exception
tags.description: Exception lifecycle, disputes, evidence, and resolutions

tags.name: Reporting
tags.description: Dashboard analytics, metrics, and synchronous report exports

tags.name: Governance
tags.description: Immutable audit logs and archive retrieval

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Service health and readiness endpoints for Kubernetes probes

tags.name: System Configs
tags.description: Runtime configuration management - view, patch, and audit system configs

tags.name: System Settings
tags.description: Tenant-scoped settings management - view, patch, and audit system settings

get.description:
Returns a simple health check response to indicate the service is alive.
Used by Kubernetes liveness probes to detect if the service needs to be restarted.

Checks if the service is ready to accept traffic by verifying all required dependencies.
Used by Kubernetes readiness probes to control traffic routing.
Returns 200 OK when all required dependencies are healthy, 503 Service Unavailable otherwise.
Dependency check details are only included in development/test environments.

200.description: Service is ready to accept traffic

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of reconciliation contexts, optionally filtered by type and status.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

200.description: List of contexts with cursor pagination

post.description: Creates a new reconciliation context used to scope matching rules.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a reconciliation context by ID.

delete.description: Removes a reconciliation context by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a reconciliation context by ID.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

post.description: Creates a deep copy of a reconciliation context including its sources, field maps, match rules, and fee rules. Referenced fee schedules are reused by cloned fee rules.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns all fee rules for a reconciliation context, ordered by priority.

post.description: Creates a new fee rule that maps transaction metadata to a fee schedule within a context. Priority must be unique within a context across all sides (LEFT, RIGHT, and ANY rules share the same priority space). The caller must also be allowed to read the referenced fee schedule.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate priority or name

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of match rules under a context, optionally filtered by type.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

200.description: List of match rules with cursor pagination

post.description: Creates a new match rule within a context.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

post.description: Reorders match rule priorities within a context.

get.description: Returns a match rule by ID.

delete.description: Removes a match rule by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a match rule by ID.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of reconciliation sources under a context, optionally filtered by type.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

200.description: List of sources with cursor pagination

post.description: Creates a new reconciliation source under a context.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a reconciliation source by ID.

delete.description: Removes a reconciliation source by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a reconciliation source by ID.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns the field map for a source within a context.
get.summary: Get a field map by source

post.description: Creates a field map for a source within a context.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a fee rule by ID.

delete.description: Removes a fee rule by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a fee rule by ID. If the referenced fee schedule changes, the caller must also be allowed to read that fee schedule.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate priority or name

delete.description: Removes a field map by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a field map by ID.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Lists export jobs for a specific reconciliation context.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Creates an async export job for large report exports (CSV, JSON, XML).

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns all schedules for a reconciliation context.

post.description: Creates a new cron-based schedule for automated matching.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a reconciliation schedule by ID.

delete.description: Removes a reconciliation schedule by ID.

patch.description: Updates fields on a reconciliation schedule.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns all discovered Fetcher database connections.
Returns a single discovered Fetcher connection by its internal ID.

post.description: Creates a tenant-scoped extraction request for a discovered connection and submits it to Fetcher.

get.description: Returns all discovered table schemas for a connection.

post.description: Tests connectivity for a specific discovered connection owned by the current tenant.

get.description: Returns extractions in the requested readiness state with cursor pagination. Drilldown surface for the operational dashboard summary. AC-F2.

parameters.description: Readiness state filter (pending, ready, stale, failed, in_flight)
parameters.name: state

parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by the previous response
parameters.name: cursor

get.description: Returns aggregate counts of Fetcher extractions partitioned by bridge readiness state (pending, ready, stale, failed, in_flight) for the requesting tenant. Powers the operational dashboard backlog widget. AC-F1.
Returns a single tenant-scoped extraction request by its internal ID.

post.description: Polls Fetcher for the latest extraction status and persists any lifecycle transition.
Forces an immediate sync with the Fetcher service, updating connections and schemas.

409.description: Refresh already in progress

get.description: Returns the current Fetcher integration status including health and connection count.
Lists all disputes with optional filters for state, category, and date range. Supports cursor-based pagination.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Retrieves a single dispute by its ID.

post.description: Closes a dispute with a resolution. The dispute can be marked as won or lost based on the outcome.
Adds evidence to a dispute. Evidence can include comments and optional file attachments to support the dispute case.

get.description: Lists all exceptions with optional filters for status, severity, assigned user, external system, and date range. Supports cursor-based pagination.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Assigns multiple exceptions to a single assignee. Partial success is supported -- each exception is processed independently.
Dispatches multiple exceptions to a target external system. Partial success is supported -- each exception is processed independently.
Resolves multiple exceptions with a shared resolution message. Partial success is supported -- each exception is processed independently.

get.description: Retrieves a single exception by its ID.

post.description: Resolves an exception by creating an adjustment entry. Used when a monetary correction is needed to reconcile the transaction.
Receives webhook callbacks from external systems to update exception status

401.description: Unauthorized - Bearer token missing or invalid

409.description: Conflict or idempotency

get.description: Retrieves all discussion comments for an exception, ordered by creation time (oldest first).

post.description: Adds a discussion comment to an exception. The author is extracted from the JWT context.

delete.description: Deletes a discussion comment from an exception.

post.description: Dispatches an exception to an external ticketing system such as JIRA or ServiceNow for tracking and resolution.

422.description: Unprocessable entity: invalid state transition or connector not configured

post.description: Opens a new dispute for an exception. Disputes are used to formally challenge or investigate discrepancies with external parties.
Resolves an exception by forcing a match with an override reason. Used when manual intervention determines the transaction should be considered matched despite discrepancies.

get.description: Retrieves the audit history for an exception, showing all actions taken on it. Pagination is forward-only (no prevCursor); use the nextCursor value to fetch subsequent pages.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description:
Lists export jobs for the authenticated tenant using forward-only cursor-based pagination.
Use the nextCursor value from the response to fetch subsequent pages.

parameters.description: Filter by status (QUEUED, RUNNING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED, EXPIRED, CANCELED)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Retrieves the status of an export job.

post.description: Cancels a queued or running export job.

409.description: Job in terminal state or idempotency conflict

get.description: Returns a presigned URL or redirects to download the export file.

409.description: Job not ready for download

get.description: Returns fee schedules for the tenant. Results may be capped by the "limit" query parameter (default 20, max 200).

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Creates a new fee schedule for transaction fee calculation.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Returns a fee schedule by ID.

delete.description: Removes a fee schedule by ID.

409.description: Conflict: fee schedule is still in use

patch.description: Updates fields on a fee schedule by ID.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

post.description: Calculates fees for a given gross amount using a specific fee schedule.

get.description: Retrieves the PII mapping for a specific actor ID.

put.description: Creates or updates the PII mapping for an actor ID. Used to associate opaque actor identifiers with human-readable display names and emails.

delete.description: Permanently removes an actor mapping for GDPR right-to-erasure compliance.

post.description: Replaces display name and email with [REDACTED] for GDPR compliance. The actor mapping record is preserved with the actor ID link intact.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of completed audit log archives for the tenant. Only archives with status COMPLETE are returned.
This endpoint uses offset-based pagination (limit/offset) rather than cursor-based pagination.
Use optional date filters (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339 format) to narrow results by archive date range.

parameters.description: Filter archives from this date (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339 format, e.g. 2024-01-01 or 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z)
parameters.name: from

parameters.description: Filter archives until this date (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339 format, e.g. 2024-12-31 or 2024-12-31T23:59:59Z)
parameters.name: to

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Generates a time-limited presigned URL for downloading a specific completed audit log archive. The URL expires after the configured presign duration.

Returns a cursor-paginated list of audit log entries with optional filters. Use this to search across all audit logs in the system. Pagination is forward-only (no prevCursor); use the nextCursor value to fetch subsequent pages.

parameters.description: Filter logs from this date (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339)
parameters.name: date_from

parameters.description: Filter logs until this date (YYYY-MM-DD or RFC3339)
parameters.name: date_to

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Retrieves a single audit log entry by its ID. Audit logs contain immutable records of all system operations for compliance and debugging purposes.

Returns a cursor-paginated list of audit log entries for a specific entity, ordered by creation time descending. Use this to trace the complete change history of any entity in the system. Pagination is forward-only (no prevCursor); use the nextCursor value to fetch subsequent pages.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns a cursor-paginated list of ingestion jobs for a reconciliation context, with optional sorting.
get.summary: List ingestion jobs for a context

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Retrieves the status and details of an ingestion job by its ID, including progress metrics and error information.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of transactions extracted from an ingestion job, with optional sorting.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Parses a sample file and returns detected column headers and sample rows. Used for field mapping configuration. Does not persist any data.

parameters.description: Sample file to preview (CSV, JSON, or XML)
parameters.name: file

parameters.description: File format (auto-detected from extension if omitted)
parameters.name: format

parameters.description: Maximum sample rows to return (default 5, max 20)
parameters.name: max_rows

parameters.description: Maximum sample rows to return (default 5, max 20)
parameters.name: max_rows

post.description: Uploads a transaction file (CSV, JSON, or XML) for ingestion into a reconciliation context. The file is parsed, validated, and transactions are extracted for matching.

409.description: Conflict: duplicate resource or idempotency key in progress

get.description: Searches transactions within a reconciliation context by text, amount range, date range, currency, source, and status. Returns offset-paginated results with total count.

parameters.description: Free text search across external ID and description
parameters.name: q

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

post.description: Marks a transaction as "Do Not Match" with a required reason. Only UNMATCHED transactions can be ignored.

409.description: Transaction already matched/ignored or idempotency conflict

post.description: Creates a balancing journal entry (e.g., bank fee, FX difference) to resolve variance between matched transactions or on a single transaction.
Triggers a matching run for a reconciliation context. Supports DRY_RUN mode for testing rules without committing results, or COMMIT mode for persisting matches.

get.description: Retrieves a list of match runs for a given reconciliation context, sorted by creation time descending.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

delete.description: Breaks an incorrect match group, rejecting it with a reason and reverting all associated transactions to UNMATCHED status.

post.description: Manually links multiple transactions (at least 2) into a match group with 100% confidence. Transactions must be UNMATCHED and belong to the specified context.

get.description: Retrieves details of a match run by its ID, including status, timing, and aggregate statistics.
Returns a cursor-paginated list of match groups from a matching run, with optional sorting. Each group contains matched transaction pairs and confidence scores.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

get.description: Returns combined dashboard aggregates including volume, match rate, and SLA statistics.
Returns unreconciled financial exposure summary with currency and age breakdowns.
Returns match rate percentage and trend data for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.
Returns complete dashboard metrics including summary, trends, and breakdowns for the Command Center.
Returns SLA compliance statistics for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.
Returns reconciliation statistics broken down by source for a context within the specified date range.
Returns transaction volume statistics for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.

Returns the total count of exceptions for the specified filters.
Used to decide between sync download (<1000 rows) and async export job.
Returns paginated matched transactions for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Sort order for created_at (asc or desc; defaults to desc)
parameters.name: sort_order

get.description: Exports matched transactions report in CSV or PDF format for the specified date range.

Returns the total count of matched records for the specified filters.
Used to decide between sync download (<1000 rows) and async export job.
Returns aggregated summary statistics for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.
Exports reconciliation summary report in CSV or PDF format for the specified date range.

Returns the total count of all transactions for the specified filters.
Used to decide between sync download (<1000 rows) and async export job.
Returns paginated unmatched transactions for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Sort order for created_at (asc or desc; defaults to desc)
parameters.name: sort_order

get.description:
Returns the total count of unmatched records for the specified filters.
Used to decide between sync download (<1000 rows) and async export job.
Exports unmatched transactions report in CSV or PDF format for the specified date range.
Returns paginated variance report rows for a reconciliation context within the specified date range.

parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Sort order for created_at (asc or desc; defaults to desc)
parameters.name: sort_order

get.description: Exports variance analysis report in CSV or PDF format for the specified date range.
Returns the service version from the VERSION environment variable (defaults to "0.0.0").

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Request payload for cloning a context with its configuration. When sources are included, their LEFT/RIGHT side assignments and field maps are preserved. When rules are included, both match rules and fee rules are cloned, and cloned fee rules keep referencing the same fee schedules as the source context.

includeRules.description: Whether to include match rules and fee rules in the clone (default: true)

includeSources.description: Whether to include sources and field maps in the clone (default: true)

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Result of cloning a reconciliation context, including counts for cloned sources, match rules, fee rules, and field maps.
Request payload for creating a reconciliation context
Request payload for creating a source inline with a context

priority.description: Priority must be unique within the context. LEFT, RIGHT, and ANY rules share the same priority space.

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Request payload for creating a field mapping configuration
Request payload for creating a matching rule
Request payload for creating a cron-based reconciliation schedule
Request payload for creating a reconciliation source

contextId.description: Context ID this field map belongs to

id.description: Unique identifier for the field map

sourceId.description: Source ID this field map is for

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp in RFC3339 format

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp in RFC3339 format

priority.description: Priority must remain unique within the context. LEFT, RIGHT, and ANY rules share the same priority space.

content.example: This transaction needs review by the finance team.

succeeded.description: IDs of exceptions that were successfully processed

resolution.example: Counterparty acknowledged the error and issued correction

exceptionId.description: Exception ID this comment belongs to

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp in RFC3339 format

dispatchedAt.description: When the dispatch occurred in RFC3339 format

externalReference.description: External reference ID from target system

exceptionId.description: Exception ID this dispute is for

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp in RFC3339 format

assignedTo.description: User the exception is assigned to

externalIssueId.description: External issue ID in the external system

externalSystem.description: External system where exception was dispatched

transactionId.description: Transaction ID this exception is for

updatedAt.description: Last update timestamp in RFC3339 format

notes.description: Additional notes explaining the force match decision
notes.example: Approved by finance team after manual review

overrideReason.description: Reason for overriding the normal matching process

changes.description: Changes made in this action (JSON object representing the diff)

createdAt.description: When the action occurred in RFC3339 format

id.description: Unique identifier for the history entry

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: List of comments for an exception

assignee.description: Assignee identifier (required when status is ASSIGNED)

dueAt.description: Due date for resolution in RFC3339 format

externalIssueId.description: Issue ID in the external system

externalSystem.description: External system identifier (e.g. JIRA, SERVICENOW, WEBHOOK)

payload.description: Arbitrary payload from the external system

status.description: Target status for the exception (e.g. ASSIGNED, RESOLVED)

updatedAt.description: External system timestamp in RFC3339 format

comment.example: Attached bank statement showing correct amount

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Actor mapping entry linking an opaque actor ID to PII

created_at.description: Timestamp when the mapping was created (RFC3339)

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the mapping was last updated (RFC3339)

date_range_end.description: End of the archived date range (RFC3339)

date_range_start.description: Start of the archived date range (RFC3339)

partition_name.description: Name of the database partition that was archived

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Immutable audit log entry for governance tracking

actorId.description: ID of the actor who performed the action

entityId.description: ID of the entity that was modified

entityType.description: Type of entity that was modified

id.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry

tenantId.description: Tenant ID that owns this audit log entry

github_com_LerianStudio_matcher_internal.description: Paginated list of audit log entries
Request body for upserting an actor mapping

contextId.description: Context ID this transaction belongs to

externalId.description: External identifier from the source system

jobId.description: Job ID that ingested this transaction

sourceId.description: Source ID this transaction belongs to

externalId.description: External identifier from the source system

jobId.description: Job ID that ingested this transaction

sourceId.description: Source ID this transaction belongs to

contextId.description: Context ID this adjustment belongs to

reason.example: Variance due to bank processing fee

database.description: Database check status: ok, down, or unknown

databaseReplica.description: DatabaseReplica check status: ok, down, or unknown

objectStorage.description: ObjectStorage check status: ok, down, or unknown

redis.description: Redis check status: ok, down, or unknown

internal_bootstrap.ReadinessResponse.description: Service readiness status with optional dependency checks

checks.description: Checks contains individual dependency status (only in development/test environments)

status.description: Status is "ok" when all required dependencies are available, "degraded" otherwise

items.description: List of reconciliation sources with field map status

internal_governance_adapters_http.Archiv.description: Presigned download URL for an archived audit log

checksum.description: SHA-256 checksum of the archive file

download_url.description: Presigned URL for downloading the archive

expires_at.description: Expiration time of the download URL (RFC3339)

internal_governance_adapters_http.ListAr.description: Paginated list of completed audit log archives

reason.example: Variance due to bank processing fee
incorrect match - amounts do not match

dateFrom.description: DateFrom is the start date for the report (YYYY-MM-DD)

dateTo.description: DateTo is the end date for the report (YYYY-MM-DD)

format.description: Format specifies the export file format (server normalizes to uppercase)

reportType.description:
ReportType specifies the type of report to export
@enum MATCHED,UNMATCHED,SUMMARY,VARIANCE,EXCEPTIONS,MATCHES,UNMATCHED_TRANSACTIONS

sourceId.description: SourceID optionally filters to a specific source

downloadUrl.description: Presigned URL to download the export file

expiresIn.description: Duration in seconds until the download URL expires

fileName.description: Original file name of the export

sha256.description: SHA-256 checksum of the file for integrity verification
